assumptions: Assume the declared dividend has created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable or payable denominated in a foreign currency.
Assume the question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities, and the context specifically notes that foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a separate top-level hedge accounting model, and the context addresses foreign currency risk on highly probable forecast intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk within a group structure.
